exceptions.reason: role is only "alert" when tone==="danger" (paired with aria-live="assertive"); every other tone -- including the default, neutral -- renders role="status" with aria-live="polite" instead. This binding stays on alert rather than moving to status on purpose, even though status is what most toasts actually render: alert is the safety-relevant contract, the one that matters for the critical/error case this component's own doc comment singles out (persist is described as "mandatory in critical/error states"), and the content.noAutoDismiss requirement below only exists to be checked against because this binds alert. Moving to status would drop that requirement from the contract entirely rather than record it as unmet, which would describe the tone that matters least and lose the one that matters most. The exception records that only the danger tone actually earns the role; every other tone is documented here as falling short of the pattern this binds, not as evidence the pattern should change.

Toast.jsx itself has no timer, but its own doc comment describes toasts as auto-dismissed by "the host" by default, and describes `persist` as "mandatory in critical/error states" to opt out. Nothing in this file enforces persist=true when tone="danger" -- that safeguard is a documented convention for the caller, not code, so a danger toast can still be rendered to disappear on a timer.
